skill.name: file-management
skill.description: Organize, back up, compress, split, and merge files/folders using rule-driven plans; use when you need safe previews, conflict handling, and verification before executing file operations.
skill.summary: Rule-driven file organization with preview, conflict handling, and verification.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: After evaluating all 88 static findings, virtually all are false positives originating from markdown documentation files. The 28 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' (HIGH) alerts are triggered by SHA256 references in PowerShell code examples used for legitimate file verification. The 38 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' and 11 'PowerShell invocation' (MEDIUM) alerts are from markdown code block formatting, not actual command execution. The single 'Hidden file access' finding is actually an exclude pattern for .git directories. One URL finding is a source attribution link. The skill is a legitimate file management guide with safe Python split/merge scripts.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Source URL
low_findings.description: Attribution URL to the source GitHub repository. This is a reference link, not a data exfiltration endpoint.

content.user_title: Organize, back up, and split files with safe previews
content.value_statement: Managing files across folders, backups, and archives is error-prone without a structured plan. This skill provides rule-driven templates and scripts to preview, organize, compress, split, and verify file operations before executing them.
actual_capabilities: Generate preview plans before moving or copying files
Organize files by extension, date, or custom rules
Back up folders with SHA256 hash verification manifests
Split large files into fixed-size parts
Merge previously split file parts back to the original
limitations: Supports Windows environments with PowerShell 5.1+ or PowerShell 7+
Does not handle file permissions, ACLs, or ownership
No built-in scheduling or automation for recurring tasks

use_cases.title: Organize a cluttered download folder
use_cases.description: Sort files from a download folder into subfolders by file type using include and exclude patterns with a safe preview step.

use_cases.title: Back up a project with integrity checks
use_cases.description: Copy project files to a backup location, generate a SHA256 hash manifest, and verify file counts and sizes.

use_cases.title: Transfer large files through size-limited channels
use_cases.description: Split a large ISO or video file into 100 MB parts for upload or email attachment, then merge on the destination side.

prompt_templates.title: Organize files by extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the file management skill to organize all PDF and DOCX files from D:\Inbox into subfolders by extension under D:\Organized. First, show me a preview plan without making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Backup with integrity verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Back up the project folder at D:\Project to E:\Backups. Include a SHA256 hash manifest and show me the file count before and after.

prompt_templates.title: Compress and archive
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress the folder D:\Project into a zip archive at E:\Archives\Project.zip using PowerShell Compress-Archive.

prompt_templates.title: Split and merge large files
prompt_templates.prompt: Split the file E:\ISO\big.iso into 100 MB parts in E:\ISO\parts using the split_file.py script, then show me how to merge them back.

output_examples.input: Show a preview of organizing my Downloads folder by file type.
Back up the project folder and verify integrity.
Split my large video file for email attachment.
output: Merge command: python merge_parts.py --input-dir parts --output demo.mp4

best_practices: Always use the preview step with -WhatIf or a dry-run mode before executing any file move or copy operation.
Generate a hash manifest for critical backups to detect data corruption during transfer or storage.
Use copy mode instead of move mode for the first pass, then switch to move after verifying the preview.
anti_patterns: Do not enable overwrite mode without reviewing the conflict report first - you may lose existing files.
Do not split files without noting the part order and original filename for correct reassembly.
Do not skip verification steps for important backups - file counts alone can miss silent corruption.

faq.question: Do I need PowerShell to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, most features require PowerShell 5.1+ or PowerShell 7+. The split and merge scripts require Python 3.8+.

faq.question: Will this skill delete my files?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses non-destructive defaults. It copies files by default and renames on conflict. Deletion requires explicit confirmation.

faq.question: Can I preview changes before they happen?
faq.answer: Yes. Every file operation supports a preview mode that shows source, destination, and action before executing.

faq.question: How does the conflict handling work?
faq.answer: When a destination file already exists, the skill renames the new file with a timestamp. Overwrite is available but only with explicit user confirmation.

faq.question: What file types can I split and merge?
faq.answer: Any file type. The split and merge scripts use raw binary read and write operations, so they work with all file formats.

faq.question: Can I schedule this to run automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for interactive use. It does not include scheduling or cron integration.
